item.title: Predictors of conversion in laparoscopic Crohn's surgery: a retrospective analysis of a tertiary cohort.
item.description: Surgical endoscopy - Laparoscopy is the preferred approach for ileocecal resection in Crohn's disease, yet conversion to open surgery remains clinically significant. This study aimed to identify independent preoperativ...

item.title: Comparative outcomes of robotic versus laparoscopic total proctocolectomy.

item.title: Robotic versus laparoscopic colectomy for deep infiltrative endometriosis of the bowel: a propensity-matched analysis of US-based hospitals.

item.title: Systematic Review: Evaluating the Prognostic Value of Granulomas in Crohn's Disease.
item.description: Digestive diseases and sciences - Review - Histological differences are among the characteristics that divide Crohn's disease (CD) from ulcerative colitis. Granulomas are one feature known to develop in a subset of patients with CD, though ...

item.title: Modernizing Aeromedical Standards for Crohn's Disease: A Risk-Stratified, Evidence-Informed Proposal for Waiver Reform.

item.title: Predictors of ileal pouch-anal anastomosis failure in ulcerative colitis: a European tertiary referral center experience.
item.description: Techniques in coloproctology - Total proctocolectomy with ileal pouch-anal anastomosis serves as the standard surgical treatment for medically refractory ulcerative colitis, yet long-term pouch failure remains a significant clin...

item.title: Preventing Crohn's recurrence, Kono-S and extended mesenteric excision: network meta-analysis category.
item.description: Langenbeck's archives of surgery - Systematic review - IntroductionCrohn's disease (CD) is a complex disease with rising incidence that is usually treated with medical management. However, surgery plays an important role for medically refractive cases,...

item.title: Utility of endoscopic ileocecal valve-plasty for Crohn's disease: a propensity-matched retrospective study.

item.title: Intestinal endometrosis mimicking Crohn's disease: a rare cause of nonspecific abdominal pain.

item.title: Intestinal ultrasound for the detection of postoperative recurrence in Crohn's disease: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: Journal of Crohn's & colitis - Meta-analysis - Postoperative recurrence of Crohn's disease (CD) is common and may occur without symptoms. Ileocolonoscopy is the reference standard for detecting endoscopic recurrence, but its invasiveness limits...
